familyNotes: These five routes are compositional shells, not atoms. Every one wraps its body in the shared `MainNavBars` (top nav + vertical nav rails + bottom overlay) and is gated by `withCoreSuspender(Component, Fallback)` - both are cross-family primitives the rewrite must keep as the outer wrapper; do NOT try to replace them with a registry layout. Four of the five (Board, Discover, Library, Search) are just a scroll container that maps a `catalogs`/`catalog` model into either `MetaRow` (horizontal poster rows) or `MetaItem` (grid cells), both of which live in the components family - so the ONLY genuinely route-specific view work here is: (1) Board's HeroCarousel, (2) Discover/Library filter bars + all-filters modal + empty/err/placeholder states, (3) Calendar's whole two-pane month view. Recurring reused data contract across all: models expose `catalog.content.type` as one of `'Loading' | 'Ready' | 'Err'` with `content.content` being either an item array, an error string, or the sentinel `'EmptyContent'`; deep-link navigation is always `navigate(toPath(deepLinks.*))` via the custom `rillio-router`, never internal state - this is the reason every dialog/menu recommendation must be URL/route-driven, not trigger-state-driven. Big cross-cutting compatibility caveat: Aceternity/Magic UI blocks almost universally assume `next/image` and Next.js app-router; they must be de-Next'd (swap `next/image` for the app's `Image` component) before use - factor that cost into every Aceternity pick. Infinite scroll everywhere uses the shared `useOnScrollToBottom` / `getVisibleChildrenRange` helpers plus a 250ms lodash.debounce; keep those, no registry part covers windowed lazy row loading. Icons are `@stremio/stremio-icons`; parts that hard-depend on lucide (shadcn defaults) need an icon swap noted per component.

components.name: Board (route shell + lazy catalog rows)
components.visualSpec: Vertical scroll page inside MainNavBars(route='board'). Order: HeroCarousel (if any item has a backdrop), then a Continue-Watching MetaRow (if items>0), then N catalog MetaRows. Each catalog row switches on content.type: 'Ready' -> MetaRow of MetaItem (className keyed to posterShape: poster/square/landscape), 'Err' non-EmptyContent -> MetaRow with a message, else -> MetaRow.Placeholder (skeleton). Rows lazy-load on scroll: getVisibleChildrenRange + a boardCatalogsOffset that accounts for hero+CW rows shifting the index map, THRESHOLD=5, 250ms debounce, re-run on layout. Responsive nth-child hiding trims visible posters per breakpoint. A floating StreamingServerWarning pins bottom when the streaming server errored and the dismissal date passed. Padding 0 1rem; hero margin .5rem 0 2rem; rows margin 1rem/2rem. Entire content fades in via 'animation-fade-in'.
components.logicToReuse: useBoard() -> [board(catalogs), loadBoardRows({start,end})]; useContinueWatchingPreview(); useStreamingServer(); useNotifications(); useProfile(); getVisibleChildrenRange; withCoreSuspender. Reused view components (other family): MetaRow (+MetaRow.Placeholder), MetaItem, ContinueWatchingItem, EventModal, MainNavBars. lodash.debounce.

recommendation.part: Keep as a custom orchestration shell. No registry 'home page' block models windowed lazy-loaded catalog rows with a posterShape-keyed skeleton/error tri-state and an index-offset for hero+CW rows. The row atom (MetaRow) is the reuse surface and belongs to the components family.
recommendation.rationale: This file is pure data-to-row mapping + scroll bookkeeping; its value is the loadBoardRows windowing contract, which is app-specific. A registry block would only replace the MetaRow atom, not this shell. Rewrite should stay ~1:1 and just consume the redesigned MetaRow.
recommendation.gaps: Registry parts cover none of: visible-range lazy loading, posterShape row variants, EmptyContent sentinel handling, streaming-server warning gating.

components.motionIdeas: Replace the CSS 'animation-fade-in' with a Framer Motion staggered container: rows animate in with opacity 0->1 + y 12->0, stagger 0.05s, so catalogs cascade as they lazy-load. Wrap the row list in AnimatePresence so newly loaded rows fade rather than pop. StreamingServerWarning: slide-up + fade from bottom (y 24->0, spring stiffness ~300 damping ~30).

components.name: HeroCarousel (3D poster coverflow hero)
components.visualSpec: Full-width banner, height clamp(18rem,46vh,34rem), radius var(--radius-card). Layered: (1) stack of backdrop cover, object-position center 25%, cross-fading on opacity 0.7s (active slide=1); (2) a left+bottom scrim gradient (color-mix on --color-bg); (3) content block bottom-left (max 40%/34rem): title logo img OR text title (2.25rem/700), 3-line-clamped description (muted), and an actions row: an accent 'Watch now' pill (bg --color-accent, play icon, brightness-110 hover) + a surface 'More info' pill (border --color-line, surface-hover on hover); (4) a right-half 3D coverflow: every poster is absolutely centered and fanned by inline transform translateX(d*58%)+translateZ(-abs*5rem)+rotateY(d*-24deg), zIndex 30-abs, side cards dimmed brightness .55 (front=1), hidden beyond VISIBLE_SIDES=3, front card links to details, side cards click-to-select; coverflow hidden below 60rem; (5) bottom-right pager dots (active dot widens to 1.5rem + accent). Auto-rotates every 7s, pauses on mouseenter, resumes on leave; index wraps mod count.
components.logicToReuse: Local state only (index, paused, setInterval 7s). Consumes item fields: background, poster, logo, name, description, deepLinks (metaDetailsStreams/player/metaDetailsVideos). Reuses rillio/components/Button (renders router-aware <a href> from deepLinks) and @stremio/stremio-icons. items come from Board's first Ready catalog.

recommendation.part: Keep custom, but rebuild the motion on Framer Motion. The 3D coverflow (per-card signed-distance translate/rotateY/translateZ) is bespoke and already matches the design; no registry carousel reproduces it. Optionally drive the autoplay/index/dots with embla-carousel-react (headless) while keeping the custom card transforms.
recommendation.rationale: Aceternity 'Apple Cards Carousel' and shadcn Carousel are flat horizontal scrollers, not a perspective coverflow, and Aceternity assumes next/image - adapting either is more work than porting the existing 40-line transform. The current component is well-built and on-brand (accent pill, dots, scrim). The only upgrade worth making is swapping hand-rolled setInterval/opacity transitions for Framer Motion springs and adding embla for robust wrap/keyboard/swipe.
recommendation.gaps: shadcn Carousel gives embla wiring + a11y + swipe but zero 3D; you supply the transforms. Aceternity apple-cards gives Apple-style layout animation but wrong shape + Next.js coupling.

components.motionIdeas: Coverflow cards: motion.div with animate={{x,rotateY,z,scale,filter}} on a spring (stiffness 260, damping 30) instead of CSS transition - gives momentum as the front card swaps. Backdrop cross-fade: AnimatePresence mode='sync', opacity + a subtle scale 1.05->1 Ken-Burns on the active backdrop over the 7s dwell. Front card entrance: slight pop (scale 0.96->1). Dots: layoutId on the active pill so the widen animates with layout. Content block: key on index, slide-up + fade the title/description on each rotate.

alternates.note: Use only as the headless index/swipe/keyboard engine under the custom card transforms; its default flat slide layout is discarded.
Wrong shape (horizontal scroll, not coverflow) and depends on next/image; reference only for the layout-animation feel.

components.name: Discover (route: filter bar + poster grid + all-filters modal)
components.visualSpec: MainNavBars(route='discover'). Top: horizontal selectable-inputs bar - up to 3 MultiselectMenu dropdowns (type, addon extras/genre, catalog), each flex-basis 15rem on --overlay-color; beyond 3 collapse into a circular 3rem filter Button (icon 'filters', surface-hover on hover, scale .95 active) that opens an all-filters ModalDialog stacking the same menus. Optional 'addon not installed' warning row with an accent Install pill opening AddonDetailsModal. Body states: catalog===null -> DelayedRenderer(500ms) empty.svg + 'no catalog selected'; content 'Err' -> empty.svg + error string; 'Loading' -> CSS-grid of CATALOG_PAGE_SIZE skeletons (poster-container padded by --poster-shape-ratio + a title bar); 'Ready' -> responsive CSS grid (repeat(10..3,1fr) by breakpoint, gap .5rem) of MetaItem, one flagged 'selected' for keyboard focus. Infinite scroll via useOnScrollToBottom (threshold 400) + auto-fill if content shorter than viewport. animation-fade-in on the grid.
components.logicToReuse: useDiscover(urlParams, queryParams) -> [discover, loadNextPage]; useSelectableInputs(discover) -> [selectInputs[], hasNextPage]; useBinaryState for the two modals; useOnScrollToBottom; CONSTANTS.CATALOG_PAGE_SIZE; useParams/useSearchParams. Reused view comps: MultiselectMenu, MetaItem, ModalDialog, AddonDetailsModal, DelayedRenderer, Image, MainNavBars. selectInputs shape: {title, options:[{value,label,title?}], value, onSelect} where onSelect navigates via toPath(deepLink).

recommendation.part: Select (for each single-value filter: type, catalog) + Dialog (all-filters overflow modal). The multiselect bar is a row of Selects; the overflow modal is a route-drivable Dialog. Keep the app's CSS grid for the poster grid (it is already a clean responsive repeat()).
recommendation.rationale: The MultiselectMenu here is effectively a labeled single-select dropdown that navigates on choose; shadcn Select (Radix, React 18.3 fine, tokens via CSS vars) maps cleanly and is the best-maintained option. Dialog is the canonical overflow-filters container and can be opened from URL state to satisfy the route-driven-layer rule. The grid itself needs no part - a registry 'bento/grid' block would fight the fixed posterShape aspect ratios.
recommendation.gaps: shadcn Select is single-value; Discover's catalog menu is conceptually single-select-that-navigates so it fits, but the addon 'extra' filters that allow a null option need the 'NONE' item wired manually. Dialog default is trigger-driven - drive open state from useBinaryState/URL instead. Neither part covers the skeleton grid, the DelayedRenderer empty states, or the auto-load-if-short-viewport logic (keep custom). Icons default to lucide - swap for stremio 'filters'.

components.motionIdeas: Grid items: Framer Motion stagger on the Ready grid (opacity+scale 0.96->1, stagger 0.02) replacing animation-fade-in; use layout so newly appended pages slot in smoothly. Filter Select content: Radix already animates; add a subtle y-2 fade via data-state variants. All-filters Dialog: scale 0.96->1 + backdrop blur-in. Skeleton->content: crossfade the skeleton grid out via AnimatePresence so posters don't pop.

alternates.note: Rich cmdk-based multiselect with search; good if a real multi-value filter is wanted. Newer Origin UI is Base UI (not Radix) - verify React 18.3 + our token theming before adopting.
Use if the catalog list grows long enough to need type-ahead search.

components.name: Library (route: type select + sort chips + grid)
components.visualSpec: MainNavBars(route=model) where model is 'library' or 'continue_watching' (chosen by withModel from pathname; unknown -> NotFound). Inputs bar: a single MultiselectMenu type filter (flex-basis 15rem, height 2.75rem, --overlay-color) + a Chips sort control (segmented pill toggles). Body: selected===null -> DelayedRenderer empty.svg + 'library not loaded' / 'CW not loaded'; catalog empty -> empty.svg + 'library empty' / 'CW empty'; else responsive CSS grid (repeat(10..3,1fr), gap .5rem) of LibItem passed {...libItem, notifications, removable: model==='library'}. Auto-scrolls to top on page-1/auth change. animation-fade-in. Redirects to first type via navigate(toPath(typeSelect.value)) when no type selected.
components.logicToReuse: useLibrary(model, urlParams, queryParams) -> [library, loadNextPage]; useSelectableInputs(library) -> [typeSelect, sortChips, hasNextPage]; useProfile; useNotifications; useOnScrollToBottom; toPath; useParams/useSearchParams/useLocation/useNavigate; withModel HOC; withCoreSuspender. Reused view comps: MultiselectMenu, Chips, LibItem, Image, DelayedRenderer, MainNavBars. sortChips shape: {options:[{value,label}], selected:[value], onSelect}.

recommendation.part: Toggle Group (single-select) for the sort Chips + Select for the type filter. Sort chips are exactly a segmented rounded-full toggle set with one active value that navigates - Toggle Group is the canonical match.
recommendation.rationale: shadcn Toggle Group (Radix, single mode) is a direct semantic and visual fit for the sort pills (our rounded-full, one-accent-active design) and themes to tokens trivially; Select mirrors the Discover type filter. Both are Radix/React-18.3 safe with no Next.js coupling. The grid stays the app's own CSS grid (no registry gain).
recommendation.gaps: Toggle Group's onValueChange gives the value; wire it to navigate(toPath(...)) rather than local state, and drive the active item from library.selectable.sorts.selected so it stays URL-derived. Default lucide icons unused here. Empty-state (empty.svg + label) and page-1 scroll-to-top are custom - keep. Consider an Empty/EmptyState primitive for the two message states.

components.motionIdeas: Toggle Group active pill: layoutId shared element so the accent background slides between sort options. Grid: same Framer stagger as Discover (opacity+scale, stagger 0.02) plus layout on LibItem so removals animate out via AnimatePresence (relevant since library items are removable). Empty state: gentle fade+float on the empty.svg (y oscillate 0->-6 loop) so the empty screen feels alive.

alternates.note: Alternative styled toggle sets if shadcn's default spacing/looks need less overriding.
If sort should read as a tab strip rather than pills; Toggle Group is the closer match to the current chips.

components.name: Search (route: query-driven catalog rows)
components.visualSpec: MainNavBars(route='search', query=<derived>) - the search INPUT itself lives in the top nav, this route only renders results. Reads query from search.selected.extra (reduceRight for name==='search'). Body: query===null -> render nothing (no landing page); catalogs empty -> empty.svg + 'no addons' message; else map catalogs to rows identical to Board: 'Ready' -> MetaRow of MetaItem keyed by posterShape, 'Err' non-EmptyContent -> MetaRow message, else MetaRow.Placeholder(title=catalogTitle). Lazy loads via getVisibleChildrenRange(THRESHOLD=100) + 250ms debounce + layout re-run. animation-fade-in per row.
components.logicToReuse: useSearch(queryParams) -> [search, loadSearchRows(range)]; getVisibleChildrenRange; useTranslate; useSearchParams; withCoreSuspender. Reused view comps: MetaRow (+Placeholder), MetaItem, Image, MainNavBars. Same catalog.content tri-state contract as Board.

recommendation.part: Keep as a custom shell (near-identical to Board's row-mapping logic, minus hero/CW). Factor the shared 'catalogs -> MetaRow tri-state' mapping into one reused helper/component so Board and Search share it. The empty state can adopt a shared Empty primitive.
recommendation.rationale: Nothing in this route is a registry candidate - it is the same windowed row mapper as Board. The only opportunity is DRY: extract the switch(content.type) row renderer so both routes and the rewrite consume it once. Search palette/input is a separate modal-route family, not here.
recommendation.gaps: No registry part for visible-range lazy loading or the EmptyContent sentinel. The 'no addons' empty screen could use a shared Empty/EmptyState block for consistency across routes.

components.motionIdeas: Rows: Framer stagger identical to Board so results cascade as the query resolves. On query change, AnimatePresence exit (fade+y) the previous result set before the new rows enter, so switching searches reads as a transition not a flash. Empty state: fade-in + subtle float on empty.svg.

components.name: Calendar (route: two-pane month view)
components.visualSpec: MainNavBars(route='calendar'). Centered content max-width 78rem, flex row gap 1rem, padding 0 1.5rem 1.5rem (0 at minimum bp). Left main column (flex auto): Selector (month nav) stacked over Table (month grid). Right: List (per-day agenda). A BottomSheet (title=selected day 'D Month', show when a day is selected) holds Details for narrow screens. Selecting a day (setSelected) highlights it across Table+List and opens the sheet. animation-fade-in on content.
components.logicToReuse: useCalendar(urlParams) -> Calendar model (selected, selectable, items[], monthInfo); useCalendarDate(profile) -> {toMonth,toDayMonth}; useProfile; useParams; withCoreSuspender. Sub-views: Selector, Table, List, Details. Reused view comp: BottomSheet (components family), MainNavBars. CalendarItem = {date:{day}, items:CalendarContentItem[]}; monthInfo={firstWeekday, today}.

recommendation.part: Keep the two-pane content-calendar layout custom (it is a media schedule grid, NOT a date-picker). Do NOT reach for shadcn Calendar / react-day-picker here - that renders a compact date input, not a full-bleed month grid with per-day episode posters. Only the BottomSheet is a registry candidate (see the Details sub-entry).
recommendation.rationale: react-day-picker models day SELECTION, not day CONTENT cells that hold scrollable poster strips and an agenda list. Forcing it would fight the whole design. The custom CSS grid (7-col week header + firstWeekday offset spacers + Cells) is the right primitive and already flat/borderless on-brand.
recommendation.gaps: shadcn Calendar covers none of: content cells, per-day poster HorizontalScroll, agenda List, or the month Selector nav. Reuse it nowhere in this route.

components.motionIdeas: Month change: AnimatePresence on the Table keyed by year-month, slide+fade the grid left/right matching prev/next direction (directional variants). Selected-day highlight: layoutId shared ring so the active outline animates between cells. List item auto-scrollIntoView already exists - pair with a highlight pulse on the active day heading.

alternates.note: Explicitly NOT a fit - date-picker, not a content month grid. Listed to record it was evaluated and rejected.

components.name: Calendar / Selector (month prev-next nav)
components.visualSpec: Horizontal bar: left Button = chevron-back icon + short prev-month label; center = big year over long current-month name; right Button = short next-month label + chevron-forward icon. Buttons navigate via navigate(toPath(deepLinks.calendar)). Bare, borderless.
components.logicToReuse: useCalendarDate(profile) -> toMonth(sel,'short'|'long'); useNavigate + toPath; selectable.prev/next.deepLinks.calendar; selected.year. Reuses rillio/components/Button + stremio-icons chevron-back/forward.

recommendation.part: Custom - a 3-slot segmented month pager. Trivial to hand-build with the app's Button + icons; no registry part is a cleaner fit than the existing ~30 lines. (A shadcn Button/Pagination could back it but adds no value.)
recommendation.rationale: It is two icon+label nav buttons around a title. The current code is already minimal and on-brand; a registry Pagination component models numbered pages, not a month stepper.
recommendation.gaps: Pagination parts assume page numbers, not prev/current/next month labels.

components.motionIdeas: On month change, crossfade+slide the center year/month label in the nav direction (x 8->0). Chevron buttons: whileTap scale 0.9, whileHover x nudge (-2 back / +2 forward). Sync direction with the Table's slide transition.

components.name: Calendar / Table + Cell (month grid of content cells)
components.visualSpec: Table: a 7-col week header (Mon..Sun, long label on wide / 3-char short on narrow) over a CSS grid; leading empty <span> spacers for monthInfo.firstWeekday, then one Cell per item. Cell: a Button with 'active'(selected day) + 'today' modifiers, a day-number heading, a HorizontalScroll strip of poster Buttons (each = play icon overlay + Image, links to deepLinks.metaDetailsStreams, poster click stops propagation and navigateWithOrigin), and a 'more-horizontal' icon when items exist. Click selects the day.
components.logicToReuse: monthInfo.firstWeekday/today; items[].date.day + items[].items[] (id,name,poster,deepLinks.metaDetailsStreams); useNavigateWithOrigin; useTranslation for weekday names. Reuses Button, HorizontalScroll, Image, stremio-icons play/more-horizontal.

recommendation.part: Custom CSS-grid month table + custom Cell. The poster strip inside each cell is a HorizontalScroll (embla could optionally back it). No registry calendar renders content-bearing day cells; keep the grid, keep HorizontalScroll (or swap to embla for momentum/snap).
recommendation.rationale: This is the core reason the whole Calendar stays custom: day cells hold scrollable poster strips + play affordances, which react-day-picker/shadcn Calendar cannot express. The existing firstWeekday-offset grid is correct and minimal.
recommendation.gaps: If richer per-cell scrolling is wanted, embla-carousel-react can replace HorizontalScroll for snap/drag; otherwise no external part applies.
recommendation.install: embla-carousel-react (optional, for the in-cell poster strip)

components.motionIdeas: Cell hover: lift (y -2) + poster brightness up. Today cell: subtle persistent accent ring pulse. Active cell: layoutId ring shared with the List selection. Poster play-icon: fade+scale in on cell hover. Grid mount: stagger cells in row-major order (opacity+scale, stagger 0.01).

components.name: Calendar / List + Item (per-day agenda)
components.visualSpec: Right-pane vertical agenda. Empty -> 3 ItemPlaceholder skeletons; else filter to days with items and render an Item per day. Item: 'active'/'today' modifiers, a day heading ('D Month'), and a body list of episode Buttons each = name + 'SxxEyy' info + play icon, linking to deepLinks.metaDetailsStreams (click stops propagation, navigateWithOrigin). Active item auto-scrollIntoView smooth on select.
components.logicToReuse: filteredItems (days with items); useCalendarDate.toDayMonth; useNavigateWithOrigin; date.day vs selected/monthInfo.today; item.items[] (id,name,season,episode,deepLinks). Reuses Button + stremio-icons play. ItemPlaceholder skeleton.

recommendation.part: Custom divide-y agenda list (matches the 'divide-y lists over cards' design rule). Rows are simple flat list items with a play affordance; the app's Button + a list wrapper suffice. No registry list part beats hand-built here.
recommendation.rationale: A borderless day-grouped agenda with skeletons is exactly the flat divide-y pattern the design language prescribes; registry 'list'/'timeline' blocks add card chrome we explicitly avoid. Keep the auto-scroll-into-view behavior.
recommendation.gaps: Kibo/registry timeline components impose vertical rails/cards not wanted. Keep placeholders custom (Skeleton primitive optional).
recommendation.install: npx shadcn@latest add skeleton (optional, for ItemPlaceholder only)

components.motionIdeas: Active day: layout highlight that slides between agenda groups (shared layoutId with the Table cell). Episode rows: stagger-in per group; whileHover reveal/scale the play icon. Skeletons: shimmer via a looping gradient x-translate. Active auto-scroll: pair the existing scrollIntoView with a brief background flash on the target group.

components.name: Calendar / Details in BottomSheet (mobile day detail)
components.visualSpec: Content of a bottom sheet (title = selected 'D Month'). Lists the selected day's videos as Buttons: name + 'SxxEyy' + play icon, href=deepLinks.metaDetailsStreams. Empty -> 'no new episodes' placeholder. Presented inside the shared BottomSheet (show + onClose), used on narrow layouts where the right List pane collapses.
components.logicToReuse: items.find(date.day===selected.day).items; useTranslation('CALENDAR_NO_NEW_EPISODES'). Reuses BottomSheet (components family) + Button + stremio-icons play. Sheet open state is driven by `selected` (setSelected/onDetailsClose) from Calendar.tsx.

recommendation.part: Drawer (vaul) - the bottom BottomSheet wrapper. shadcn Drawer with direction='bottom' + snap points is the canonical vaul-backed bottom sheet, matching the current iOS-style sheet. Note the sheet itself is the shared BottomSheet component (components family); this entry's recommendation is what that primitive should be rebuilt on.
recommendation.rationale: vaul/shadcn Drawer is the standard for exactly this pattern (drag-dismiss bottom sheet, Radix Dialog under the hood, React 18.3 + tokens fine) and supports being opened from external/URL-derived state (show prop) rather than a DrawerTrigger, satisfying the route-driven-layer rule. Details' inner content stays a custom flat video list.
recommendation.gaps: Drive Drawer open from the `selected` state (controlled open/onOpenChange), not a DrawerTrigger. Default lucide grabber/close - keep bare per our icon-button convention. The inner video list + 'no new episodes' empty stay custom. Confirm vaul version's Radix peer works on React 18.3 (it does).

components.motionIdeas: Drawer already springs up (vaul); add snap points (peek + full). Inner video rows: stagger-in after the sheet settles. Empty 'no new episodes': fade + slight scale. whileTap on each video row (scale 0.98) for tactile feedback.

alternates.note: Radix-Dialog side sheet - use if a non-draggable edge panel is preferred over a draggable bottom sheet on tablet widths.
